description: This figure characterizes the microstructure of M2-IA sample, which is Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C low-density medium Mn steel after intercritical annealing at 700℃ for 1 h followed by water quenching. Subfigures a1 to a4 are scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images with gradually increasing magnification, corresponding to scale bars of 200 μm, 20 μm, 5 μm, and 3 μm respectively, identifying phases including δ-ferrite, α-ferrite, and martensite-austenite (α'+γ) constituent. Subfigure b is an electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) phase map with a scale bar of 5 μm, where the orange region represents austenite and the white region represents ferrite phases. The characterization confirms that the Si-added M2 steel has a tri-phase microstructure of δ-ferrite, α-ferrite, and austenite after intercritical annealing, supporting the conclusion that Si addition promotes δ-ferrite precipitation, adjusts phase composition, and further influences austenite stability in medium Mn steels.
